MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON APR-04-2017 IN 206, PLAINTIFF ROSEMARY D'ALESSANDRO'S MOTION FOR TRIAL PREFERENCE UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 36(A) WAS HEARD. ARGUED AND THE COURT GRANTED THE MOTION FOR PREFERENCE PURSUANT TO CCP 36(A). THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 07/03/17 AT 9:30AM IN DEPARTMENT 206. LAST DAY IS 08/02/17. PLAINTIFF WAIVED A JURY TRIAL AND DEFENDANT HAS UNTIL 4:00PM ON 04/05/17 TO POST JURY FEES, OTHERWISE THE MATTER WILL BE SET AS A COURT TRIAL. DISCOVERY AS SET FORTH/STATED IN WRITTEN ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, MATTER NOT REPORTED (206/TLJ)
REPLY OF DEFENDANT JEAN MING YU, M.D. (TRANSACTION ID # 100013890) FILED BY DEFENDANT YU MD, JEAN MING

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT JEAN MING YU'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. DR. YU FAILED TO MEET HER BURDEN OF ESTABLISHING THE EXISTENCE OF A VALID AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE. THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT IS SUBMITTED AS AN EXHIBIT TO AN ATTORNEY DECLARATION BUT HAS NOT BEEN PROPERLY AUTHENTICATED. PLAINTIFF ROSEMARY D'ALESSANDRO'S OBJECTION TO PARAGRAPH FIVE OF THE SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION OF ALEXANDER MACDOUGALL (WHICH IS SUBSTANTIALLY THE SAME AS PARAGRAPH 3 TO HIS ORIGINAL DECLARATION) IS SUSTAINED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: STEPHEN MURPHY; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR #8189 =302/HK

Petition Of Defendant Jean Ming Yu, M.D. To Compel Arbitration And Stay Court Proceedings
Off Calendar Jun-02-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. GEOFFREY SPELLBERG, TEL. NO. 1-415-678-3817 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DAVID A. DEPOLO & ATTY. MATTHEW H. SMITH O THE DONNELLY NELSON & MURRAY, TEL. NO. 1-925-287-8181 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Jean Ming Yu's petition to compel arbitration is granted and this case is stayed pending conclusion of the arbitration proceedings. This second motion to compel arbitration is not a motion for reconsideration. Per the court's April 18, 2017 ruling, the previous motion was denied without prejudice. Dr. Yu has satisfied her burden of showing the existence of a valid arbitration agreement. Late-filed reply evidence aside, Ms. Hancock and Dr. Morey Filler's declarations taken together are sufficient to authenticate the arbitration agreement. Per Cochran v. Rubens (1996) 42 Cal.App.4th 481, 485, Ms. D'Alessandro had an open book account with the medical group at the time of the surgical procedures due to the parties' expectations of possible future transactions. Ms. D'Alessandro has not shown the arbitration agreement is so substantively and procedurally unconscionable as to render the agreement unenforceable. Dr. Yu has not waived her contractual right to arbitration by acting inconsistently given the accelerated trial date .The court also ruled on the Evidentiary objections as follows: Objection #1-Sustained; Obj. #2 -Overruled; Obj. #3- Sustained;
Obj #4- Overruled; Obj # 5-overruled and Obj #6 - Overruled. Order signed in open court.
Judge: Stephen Murphy; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Irene Tofft, CSR # 12313

Notice Of Hearing On Petition Of Defendant Jean Ming Yu, M.D. To Compel Arbitration And Stay Court Proceedings
Off Calendar Apr-18-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. GEOFFREY SPELLBERG OF THE SPELLBERG LAW OFFICES, TEL. NO. 1-415-678-3817 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DAVID A. DEPOLO OF THE DONNELLY NELSON DEPOLO & MURRAY, TEL. NO. 1-925-287-8181 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Jean Ming Yu's motion to compel arbitration is denied without prejudice. Dr. Yu failed to meet her burden of establishing the existence of a valid agreement to arbitrate. The arbitration agreement is submitted as an exhibit to an attorney declaration but has not been properly authenticated. Plaintiff Rosemary D'Alessandro's objection to paragraph five of the Supplemental Declaration of Alexander MacDougall (which is substantially the same as paragraph 3 to his original declaration) is sustained. Order signed in open court. Judge: Stephen Murphy; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk
